channel.title: Microsoft Cloud Platform Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily updates from Azure and Microsoft 365 blogs

item.title: Logic Apps Newsletter - May 2026
item.description: In this issue: Ace Aviator of the Month News from our product group News from our community Ace Aviator of the Month May 2026's Ace Aviator: Yahya Ajwad Yahya Ajwad - Chief Architect Cloud Integration @ Epical What's your role and title? What are your responsibilities? I work as Chief Architect and...
item.dc:creator: WSilveira

item.title: Performance Tuning and Scaling Optimization for Large-Scale Azure Workloads
item.description: Summary As cloud-native systems scale, performance challenges rarely stem from a single bottleneck. Instead, they emerge from the interaction between compute, orchestration, and data layers under load. This article captures a practical optimization journey of a high-volume Azure-based workload and...
item.dc:creator: ruchitapradhan

item.title: Running GitHub Actions Runners on Azure Container Apps with KEDA Autoscaling
item.description: GitHub-hosted runners work well for most scenarios. But as workloads grow, teams often need: Better cost optimization — pay only when jobs run More control over execution environments and installed tools Scalable parallel execution — run 10, 20, or 50 jobs simultaneously Network access to private...
item.dc:creator: shubhijain

item.title: From Drift to Self-Healing: Building a Multi-Repo Azure AI Infrastructure You Can Actually Trust
item.description: A question before we begin Quick — what version of infrastructure is running in your production subscription right now? Not what your pipeline last deployed. Not what your tracking spreadsheet says. What's actually running — right now, this second — and can you prove it? If you had to think about...
item.dc:creator: Valini_Sunthwal

item.title: High Availability Testing for Azure Kubernetes Service in a Single Region with Availability Zones
item.description: During migration testing engagements for enterprise workloads moving to Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS), we frequently validate high availability behavior under simulated infrastructure failures within a single Azure region using Availability Zones. In one such workload migration scenario involving...
item.dc:creator: jiteshhp

item.title: Announcing the public preview of Oracle Database built-in connector for Azure Logic Apps Standard
item.description: Announcing the public preview of Oracle Database built-in connector for Azure Logic Apps Standard Run Oracle Database operations natively in your Logic Apps Standard workflows. Today we’re excited to announce the public preview of Oracle Database built-in connector for Azure Logic Apps (Standard)....
item.dc:creator: Shree_Divya_M_V

item.title: Removing The Monkey Work of Migration
item.description: Removing The Monkey Work of Migration; in this post we show how Git-Ape analyses an AWS deployment repo and generates an Azure-native replacement, with design critique built in. This post walks through a real migration workflow: start with an AWS deployment repo and end with an Azure deployment...
item.dc:creator: davidwright, Arnaud Lheureux, Suzanne Daniels

item.title: Running GitHub Actions Runners on Azure Container Apps with KEDA Autoscaling
item.description: GitHub-hosted runners work well for most scenarios. But as workloads grow, teams often need: Better cost optimization — pay only when jobs run More control over execution environments and insta...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Container Apps, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: How MS Discovery Is Empowering Scientists to Do More
item.description: Research and development has traditionally been a slow, sequential, and largely manual endeavour. Scientists formulate hypotheses, design experiments, run computations in constrained environments, and document results, each stage dependent on the last, each transition requiring human review and...
item.dc:creator: sameeraman

item.title: Automating Azure Naming Standards using API and DevOps Pipelines
item.description: Introduction In large Azure environments, one of the most overlooked yet critical governance challenges is resource naming consistency. While organizations define naming standards, enforcing them at scale across multiple subscriptions, teams, and pipelines often becomes a manual and inconsistent...
item.dc:creator: sameenamohammed

item.title: Modernizing Terraform Pipelines on Azure: OIDC Federation for GitHub Actions and Azure DevOps
item.description: The secret nobody wants to rotate Most Terraform-on-Azure pipelines we see still authenticate the same way they did three years ago. A long-lived ARM_CLIENT_SECRET sitting in GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps, set once, copied around, and rotated only when something breaks. It's the most ignored...
item.dc:creator: ssinghkalra

item.title: Automating Azure Naming Standards using API and DevOps Pipelines
item.description: Introduction In large Azure environments, one of the most overlooked yet critical governance challenges is resource naming consistency. While organizations define naming standards, enforcing them...Update Type: Announcement, Services: , Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Reimagining Azure Governance with Automation & EPAC
item.description: 🧩 The Challenge: Governance at Scale Managing Azure environments manually introduces: ❌ Policy drift across subscriptions ❌ Inconsistent naming conventions ❌ Delays in compliance enforcement ❌ Human errors in deployments 🔍 Insight: Governance gaps are often not due to lack of policies—but lack of...
item.dc:creator: sameenamohammed

item.title: Update: Egress and data transfer with Azure in the United Kingdom
item.description: We support customer choice, including the choice to transfer your data between Azure and environments outside of Azure. Azure has offered free egress and at-cost data transfer for customers and CSP partners in Europe transferring data via the internet between Azure to another data processing...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: High-Fidelity Network Observability at Scale— ACNS Metrics Filtering and Log Aggregation Now GA
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that Advanced Container Networking Services (ACNS) for Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now delivers two powerful observability features in General Availability: container network metrics filtering and container network log filtering and aggregation. Together, these...
item.dc:creator: chandanAggarwal

item.title: The new Run dialog: faster, cleaner, and more capable
item.description: We’ve shipped the new Run dialog – and we want to say thanks. As the team behind Windows Terminal and PowerToys, we’re excited to bring this to Windows 11, and your feedback helped shape it! Run has been rebuilt from the ground up: Modern design: A refreshed look that matches Fluent Design and...
item.dc:creator: Clint Rutkas

item.title: Shift-Left Governance for AI Agents: How the Agent Governance Toolkit Helps You Catch Violations
item.description: In part one of this series, we covered AGT’s runtime governance: the policy engine, zero-trust identity, execution sandboxing, and the OWASP Agentic AI risk mapping. That post focused on what happens when an agent acts: policy evaluation at the moment a tool call fires, trust scoring when agents...
item.dc:creator: mosiddi

item.title: Introducing langchain-azure-cosmosdb: Build Agentic Apps and RAG with One Database
item.description: Build AI Agents and RAG Applications with the New LangChain + LangGraph Connector for Azure Cosmos DB Building AI agents and RAG applications today means stitching together half a dozen services, a vector database, a chat history store, a checkpointer for agent state, a semantic cache, a long-term...
item.dc:creator: James Codella, Aayush Kataria

item.title: Protect Your Streaming Data in Use: Confidential Computing for Azure Event Hubs Dedicated
item.description: For organizations in regulated industries — financial services, healthcare, government, defense — the security conversation around data has always revolved around two states: data at rest and data in transit. Encrypt your storage. Encrypt your network traffic. Check, check. But there's a third...
item.dc:creator: ashish-chhabria

item.title: AI‑Accelerated AVM Refactoring: Modernizing Legacy IaC Safely and Swiftly
item.description: Why AVM refactoring is harder in brownfield Greenfield AVM adoption is straightforward: pick the module, deploy, and iterate. Brownfield is different. You’re refactoring *live* infrastructure — where state, naming conventions, diagnostic settings, and policy baselines already exist. In one...
item.dc:creator: HimanshuYadav

item.title: From Terminal to Autonomous Coding: Mastering GitHub Copilot CLI ACP Server
item.description: Introduction The rise of AI-powered development is no longer just about autocomplete—it’s about autonomous agents that can think, act, and collaborate. At the center of this transformation is the Agent Client Protocol (ACP) and its integration with GitHub Copilot CLI. If you’ve ever wanted to:...
item.dc:creator: Ravindra_Kumar_Vishwakarma

item.title: Azure Developer CLI (azd) – April 2026
item.description: The Azure Developer CLI (azd) shipped five releases in April 2026. The biggest theme this month is multi-language hook support: write azd hooks in Python, JavaScript, TypeScript, or .NET alongside the existing Bash and PowerShell options. Here’s what’s in versions 1.23.14, 1.23.15, 1.24.0, 1.24.1,...
item.dc:creator: Kristen Womack

item.title: AHEAD helps us launch the Strategic Azure Storage Services Partner Program
item.description: Authors: Aung Oo, Vice President Azure Storage Product Management Parker Leavitt, Partner Account Manager, AHEAD Special thanks to: Michael Johnson, Vice President, Cloud, AHEAD AHEAD background AHEAD was formed in 2007 and possesses deep expertise in Infrastructure, Cloud Platforms, AI, Analytics,...
item.dc:creator: karautenMSFT

item.title: Introducing DeepSeek V4 Flash and V4 Pro in Microsoft Foundry
item.description: As AI adoption matures, the conversation is shifting from model capability to system design, how to orchestrate models that deliver the right balance of quality, speed, and cost. Today, we’re expa...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure AI Services, Microsoft Foundry, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Prompt flow is being retired
item.description: Prompt Flow in Microsoft Foundry and Azure Machine Learning will be retired on 20 April 2027. Customers should begin migrating existing Prompt Flow applications and deployments to Microsoft Agent Fra...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Machine Learning, Microsoft Foundry, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: What’s new in Microsoft Sentinel: April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. April brings a broad set of updates, with RSAC 2026 announcements rolling out alongside new features. Highlights include cost limit enforcement to prevent runaway query costs, curated open-source intelligence in Threat...
item.dc:creator: vkokkengada

item.title: Whats new in Microsoft Sentinel: April 2026
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 edition of What's new in Microsoft Sentinel. April brings a broad set of updates, with RSAC 2026 announcements rolling out alongside new features. Highlights include cost li...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Microsoft Sentinel, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Explaining what GitHub Copilot Modernization can (and cannot do)
item.description: In the last post, we looked at the workflow: assess, plan, execute. You get reports you can review and the agent makes changes you can inspect. If you don’t know, GitHub Copilot Modernization is the new agentic tool that supports you to in modernizing older applications. Could it support you with...
item.dc:creator: PabloLopes

item.title: Azure NCv6 Virtual Machines: Enhancements and GA Transition
item.description: NCv6 Virtual Machines are Azure's flexible, next generation platform enabling both leading-edge graphics and generative AI compute workloads. Featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition (BSE) GPUs, Intel Xeon™ 6 "Granite Rapids" 6900P series CPUs, and a suite of Microsoft Azure...
item.dc:creator: Fernando_Aznar

item.title: Generally Available: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS
item.description: Support for prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS for Azure Blob Storage is generally available in all Azure regions. SAS tokens for Blob Storage have historically supported two levels of scope: container and individual blob. With this release, you can now scope access to a prefix or virtual...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS
item.description: Support for prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS for Azure Blob Storage is generally available in all Azure regions. SAS tokens for Blob Storage have historically supported two levels of scope: container and individual blob. With this release, youUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Storage,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description: Azure Functions support for Java 25 is now generally available. You can now develop apps using Java 25 locally and deploy them to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including the Flex Consumption plan. Learn more: Update your app to Java 25 Learn more about Java 25 Azure...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Functions support for Java 25
item.description: Azure Functions support for Java 25 is now generally available. You can now develop apps using Java 25 locally and deploy them to supported Azure Functions plans on Linux and Windows, including the Flex Consumption plan. Learn more: Update your appUpdate Type: GA, Services: Azure Functions,...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: TypeScript 7 Beta Now Enabled by Default in Visual Studio 2026 18.6 Insiders 3
item.description: TypeScript 7 Beta Now Enabled by Default in Visual Studio 2026 18.6 Insiders 3 In Visual Studio 2026 18.6 Insiders 3 we have updated the built-in TypeScript SDK to TypeScript 7 Beta (native preview). The TypeScript SDK provides the compiler and language service used for TypeScript and JavaScript...
item.dc:creator: Sayed Ibrahim Hashimi

item.title: Prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS is now generally available for Azure Blob Storage
item.description: We would like to share that prefix-scoped access for User Delegation SAS for Azure Blob Storage is generally available in all Azure regions. SAS tokens for Blob Storage have historically supported two levels of scope: container and individual blob. With this release, you can now scope access to a...
item.dc:creator: despindola

item.title: Introducing Post-Stream Refinement: Higher-Accuracy Real-Time Transcription
item.description: Real-time speech recognition has always been defined by a fundamental trade-off: speed versus accuracy. Applications that need instant, streaming transcription — live captions, voice agents, real-tim...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Microsoft Foundry, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Voice Live API now supports WebRTC (Preview)
item.description: We are thrilled to announce that Voice Live API now supports WebRTC (Web Real-Time Communication) connection, enabling low‑latency, real‑time voice interactions directly from web and mobile clients. ...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Microsoft Foundry, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Public Preview: Managed Identity support for graphical session recording
item.description: Overview Azure Bastion provides secure RDP and SSH access to Azure virtual machines directly via the Azure portal or via the native SSH/RDP client already installed on your local computer. Today, we are introducing public preview for managed identity support for session recording, giving...
item.dc:creator: aarontsang

item.title: Discover Fabric items across workspaces with the OneLake Catalog Search API, MCP and CLI tools
item.description: The OneLake Catalog helps people find, explore, and curate Fabric items across their organization. With OneLake Catalog Search API, MCP and CLI tools, that same discovery experience is now available programmatically, making it possible to build search directly into scripts, internal tools, and...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Azure Data Studio to VS Code: What it means for SQL database in Fabric developers
item.description: Azure Data Studio (ADS) has been a familiar tool for SQL developers, but with its retirement on February 6, 2025, and end of support on February 28, 2026, teams need a clear path forward. For those working with SQL database in Microsoft Fabric, the transition to Visual Studio Code (VS Code) is not...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Pipelines are evolving beyond ETL
item.description: For years, data pipelines have been synonymous with ETL (Extract, Transform, Load): extract data from one place, transform it, and load it somewhere else. That model worked well when analytics workflows were mostly batch driven and isolated from the rest of the business. But that world has changed....
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Fabric April 2026 Feature Summary
item.description: Welcome to the April 2026 Microsoft Fabric update! This month’s update brings a broad set of new capabilities across Microsoft Fabric, spanning the platform experience, Data Engineering, Data Science, Data Warehouse, and Real-Time Intelligence. Read on to learn about improvements to the Fabric...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: New Local Management Group for ALZ & Updated Sovereign Policies for SLZ
item.description: Following on from months of working alongside customers, partners, and our internal product groups, we have now made two updates to the Azure landing zone (ALZ) and the Sovereign landing zone (SLZ), that I’d like to walk you through in this post: A new dedicated ‘Local’ Management Group added...
item.dc:creator: jtracey93msft

item.title: 🚀 From Drift to Diagnosis: AI‑Powered Root Cause Analysis for Azure Infrastructure
item.description: 🌍 A Real-World Scenario During a recent production deployment of an enterprise AI platform, everything looked perfectly aligned from an infrastructure perspective: ✅ Infrastructure deployed via IaC (Terraform)✅ Private endpoints enforced✅ Public access disabled for all AI services A few hours...
item.dc:creator: Pooja_Pradhan

item.title: 🖼️Streamline Image Generation Workflow in Foundry Toolkit
item.description: Integrating image generation into a production AI application has historically meant juggling multiple surfaces — browsing models in the Foundry portal, setting up deployments via the Azure CLI, testing prompts in a separate tool, then stitching together API credentials before writing a single line...
item.dc:creator: junjieli

item.title: Fixing Broken Markdown in AI Translation: Hardening a Production Pipeline
item.description: By Minseok Song and Hiroshi Yoshioka (Microsoft MVPs) TL;DR Recent community feedback, especially from Japanese translations, revealed that many translation failures were not semantic, but structural. Through detailed issue reports and discussions, we identified recurring patterns such as broken...
item.dc:creator: MinseokSong

item.title: Propagating SharePoint Document Permissions to AI Search and RAG Pipelines
item.description: A security-first architecture that propagates SharePoint document permissions into downstream AI systems, using Microsoft Entra ID object IDs (GUIDs) for safe, query-time filtering in Azure AI Search, RAG pipelines, and Copilot extensions. The post Propagating SharePoint Document Permissions to AI...
item.dc:creator: Elena Vavilova

item.title: From ServiceNow to Self-Healing Infrastructure: A Multi-Repo Azure AI Platform
item.description: How we built a self-verifying Azure platform that deploys infrastructure across multiple markets, tracks every change with three independent records, and catches drift, whether it comes from a failed...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure AI Services, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: From Drift to Self-Healing: Building a Multi-Repo Azure AI Infrastructure You Can Actually Trust
item.description: How we built a self-verifying Azure platform that deploys infrastructure across multiple markets, tracks every change with three independent records, and catches drift, whether it comes from a failed...Update Type: Announcement, Services: Azure AI Services, Categories:
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Running Diffusion Models at Scale on AKS
item.description: Diffusion workloads are simple at prototype scale and unforgiving in production. A single demo can run on one GPU-backed VM, but a real platform has to handle bursty demand, long-running jobs, model artifact distribution, secure public access, rollout safety, and hardware-level observability. Azure...
item.dc:creator: PrabalDeb
